reasoning: Viswanathan Anand's FIDE rating on 2025-12-07 is 2743. A 1% increase would require his rating to reach at least 2771. Anand is largely inactive in classical chess, focusing on his role as FIDE Deputy President (such as actively participating in closing ceremonies rather than playing in the recent World Cup). FIDE ratings are updated on the 1st of each month. Therefore, on December 14, his rating will undoubtedly remain exactly 2743 (the December 1 rating), dictating a near-zero probability. For January and March 2026, he would have to re-enter competitive classical play and gain 28 points against super-GMs, a feat that requires a massive, sustained overperformance nearly impossible for a semi-retired 55-year-old. His rating has been flat at 2743 for months and will very likely stay there. The probability is strictly set at the floor (0.02) for the nearest dates, bumping marginally to 0.03 for March 2026 to reflect slightly higher uncertainty purely over the longer time horizon.
